headline: Move better.
Get stronger.
Get back on your feet.
hero.lead: 22 Strong Foundation helps veterans, student athletes, and others access hands-on recovery care when insurance, cost, or other barriers get in the way.
hero.credit: Care is delivered through a local Orange County clinic for people who need help recovering from pain, injury, or physical setbacks.

veterans.text: veterans report chronic pain after service. When pain is part of daily life, recovery care should not be treated as optional.
of post-9/11 veterans have not used VA health care since leaving the service.

athletes.text: of high school sports injuries can end a season. The right recovery support can protect more than a roster spot.

others.text: is the typical cost of one outpatient physical-therapy session for someone paying without insurance.
a year is a common insurance cap on physical therapy — often less than recovery actually takes.

luke.story: Luke shares why access to hands-on recovery care matters after service.

adam.quote: “A major part of my success was the treatments and therapy I received from Dr. Vas.”
adam.attribution: Adam Folker · Collegiate & Professional Basketball Player
adam.story: During my sophomore year in college, I had hip surgery that was potentially season-ending and even career-ending. My coaches referred me to Dr. Vas, who was able to take care of me and eventually get me back to playing within a short few weeks. That season I ended up winning hustle player of the year award. Throughout my career, I saw Dr. Vas on various occasions for injuries that came up, along with maintenance and preventive care. I was able to play competitively at the top level.

strongCircle.heading: Monthly support that keeps care moving.
strongCircle.lead: The Strong Circle is the monthly giving community that helps 22 Strong plan care before a recovery gap turns into a stopped plan.
strongCircle.subhead: A steady $22 helps us say yes faster
strongCircle.copy: One-time gifts open the door. Monthly gifts create the reliable base behind the next appointment.
strongCircle.cardTitle: Build the monthly base behind future recovery plans.
strongCircle.cardBody: Recurring gifts help 22 Strong plan ahead for people referred by providers, coaches, veterans, and community partners.
strongCircle.miniBody: A monthly gift gives the foundation a steadier way to fund care beyond the first appointment.

modal.heading: Join the Strong Circle.
modal.lead: A steady monthly gift gives 22 Strong a reliable base for appointments, follow-through, and urgent care gaps before someone has to ask twice.

proof.label: Steady support
proof.value: Funds care beyond intake and the first visit.

leadership.name: Dr. Vasili “Dr. Vas” Gatsinaris, D.C.
paragraphs: Dr. Vas earned his Doctorate of Chiropractic from the Los Angeles College of Chiropractic in 2001 and has more than 25 years of experience helping people recover and rebuild.

Over his career, he has worked with everyone from professional athletes to first responders, developing a hands-on, integrative approach focused on restoring mobility and getting people back to life stronger and faster.

He is co-owner of Next Level Fitness & Wellness Center in Newport Beach, a full-service wellness facility offering chiropractic, physical therapy, massage therapy, and nutrition — the same recovery care 22 Strong helps fund.

leadership.name: Travis Mayfield
paragraphs: A Purple Heart Army veteran who served with the 101st Airborne, Travis is Co-Owner and President of Next Level Fitness & Wellness Center. He helped bring 22 Strong together and leads its mission to make recovery care accessible to those who served and to others working to rebuild.
His own path from service informs how 22 Strong shows up for the people it serves: with respect, follow-through, and care that doesn’t stop at the first appointment.

donate.heading: Fund the next $22,000 in recovery care.
donate.lead: Every gift helps turn a stalled recovery plan into appointments someone can actually reach.
donate.outcomes: Since 2024, donor support has funded over 150 recovery sessions for 18 people across Orange County.
donate.goalCopy: $22,000 covers roughly 115 recovery sessions — enough to help 10–15 people stay with a complete care plan.
